rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Use implementation annexes only for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Align helper declaration types with expression types to avoid type mismatch errors
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the most specific valid scope that contains all referenced identifiers
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as ports or constants
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope; otherwise omit the clause
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names in assumptions and guarantees
For output dependency contracts, relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
